content: Just as the linguistic meaning of "reflexion" encompasses both literal light reflection off a surface and deliberate cognitive self-reflection in human learning, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to mirror this dual function: not only does the LLM critique its own previous outputs to improve performance, but it also "bounces" off inconsistent solution perspectives (as documented in Self-Contrast research) to generate a clearer reflected representation of the problem itself, similar to how a surface reflects light to render a visible image. This dual framing suggests LLM reflection can be split into two complementary, sequential stages rather than treated as a single monolithic process.
